steps.title: Use the Pythagorean theorem
steps.content:
Let $h$ be the hypotenuse. The legs are $27$ and $24$, so
$$h^2=27^2+24^2$$

steps.title: Simplify the hypotenuse
steps.content:
Compute the squares and factor out a perfect square.
$$h^2=729+576=1{,}305$$
$$h=\sqrt{1{,}305}=\sqrt{9\cdot145}=3\sqrt{145}$$

steps.title: Match the given form
steps.content: The hypotenuse has the form $3\sqrt{d}$, so $d=145$. The answer is 145.
